NCT02456740.name: STRIVE
NCT02456740.group: Episodic migraine (4-14 MMD), erenumab 70/140 mg SC monthly vs placebo

allOutcomes.title: Change in monthly migraine days months 4-6 (placebo-corrected MD)
Proportion with 50% reduction in MMD (responder rate, 140 mg vs placebo)

NCT02456740.snippet: Source: Goadsby PJ et al. NEJM 2017;377:2123-2132 (STRIVE).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Goadsby PJ et al. NEJM 2017;377:2123-2132, CONSORT
evidence.text: 955 adults with episodic migraine (4-14 migraine days/month) randomized 1:1:1 to erenumab 70 mg SC monthly (n=317), erenumab 140 mg SC monthly (n=319), or placebo (n=319) for 6 months.

evidence.label: Primary — Change in Monthly Migraine Days
evidence.source: Goadsby PJ et al. NEJM 2017, Figure 2
evidence.text: Change in MMD months 4-6: -3.2 with erenumab 70 mg, -3.7 with 140 mg, -1.8 with placebo. Placebo-corrected MD: -1.4 (70 mg) and -1.9 (140 mg); both P<0.001. Pooled active vs placebo MD approx -1.7.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* History of migraine (with or without aura) for ≥ 12 months prior to screening according to the International Headache Society (IHS) International Classification of Headache Disorders (ICHD-3) classification~* Migraine frequency: ≥ 4 and < 15 migraine days per month on average across the 3 months prior to screening and during baseline~* Headache frequency: < 15 headache days per month on average across the 3 months prior to screening and baseline~* Demonstrated at least 8...

NCT02614183.name: EVOLVE-1
NCT02614183.group: Episodic migraine, galcanezumab 120/240 mg SC monthly vs placebo (US)

allOutcomes.title: Change in monthly migraine days months 1-6 (placebo-corrected MD, 120 mg)

NCT02614183.snippet: Source: Stauffer VL et al. JAMA Neurol 2018;75:1080-1088 (EVOLVE-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Stauffer VL et al. JAMA Neurol 2018
evidence.text: 858 adults with episodic migraine in North America randomized 1:1:2 to galcanezumab 120 mg (n=213), galcanezumab 240 mg (n=212), or placebo (n=433). 6-month double-blind treatment.

evidence.label: Primary — Change in Monthly Migraine Days
evidence.source: Stauffer VL et al. JAMA Neurol 2018, Figure 2
evidence.text: Mean change in MMD months 1-6: -4.7 galcanezumab 120 mg, -4.6 galcanezumab 240 mg, -2.8 placebo. Placebo-corrected MD: -1.9 (120 mg; P<0.001), -1.8 (240 mg; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have a diagnosis of episodic migraine as defined by International Headache Society (IHS) International Classification of Headache Disorders (ICHD)-3 beta guidelines (1.1 or 1.2) (ICHD-3 2013), with a history of migraine headaches of at least 1 year prior to screening, migraine onset prior to age 50 and MONTHLY frequency of 4-14 Migraine Headache Days (MHD).~Exclusion Criteria:~* Are currently enrolled in or have participated within the last 30 days or within 5 half-lives (...

NCT02629861.name: HALO-EM
NCT02629861.group: Episodic migraine, fremanezumab monthly/quarterly vs placebo

allOutcomes.title: Change in monthly migraine days weeks 1-12 (placebo-corrected MD, monthly dosing)
Proportion with 50% reduction in MMD (monthly 225 mg)

NCT02629861.snippet: Source: Dodick DW et al. JAMA 2018;319:1999-2008 (HALO-EM).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 875 adults with episodic migraine randomized 1:1:1 to fremanezumab monthly (225 mg monthly, n=290), fremanezumab quarterly (675 mg at baseline plus placebo, n=291), or placebo monthly (n=294) for 12 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary — Change in Monthly Migraine Days
evidence.text: Mean change in MMD weeks 1-12: -3.7 monthly dosing, -3.4 quarterly, -2.2 placebo. Placebo-corrected MD: -1.5 (monthly; P<0.001), -1.3 (quarterly; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 70 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Males or females aged 18 to 70 years, inclusive, with migraine onset at ≤50 years of age~* Patient signs and dates the informed consent document~* Patient has history of migraine according to International Classification of Headache Disorders, or clinical judgment suggests a migraine diagnosis~* 85% e-diary compliance~* Total body weight between 99 and 265 lbs, inclusive~  * Additional criteria apply, please contact the investigator for more information~Exclusion Criteria:...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | CGRP Monoclonal Antibodies for Episodic Migraine Prevention v1.0
